en.extract:
The Wetar scops owl (Otus tempestatis) is an owl endemic to Wetar of the Maluku Islands of Indonesia. It was previously considered a subspecies of the Moluccan scops owl (O. magicus), but was split as a distinct species by the IUCN Red List and BirdLife International in 2014, and the International Ornithological Congress followed suit in 2022.

The '''Wetar scops owl''' ('''''Otus tempestatis''''') is an owl [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Wetar]] of the Maluku Islands of [[Indonesia]]. It was previously considered a subspecies of the [[Moluccan scops owl]] (''O. magicus''), but was split as a distinct species by the [[IUCN Red List]] and [[BirdLife International]] in 2014, and the [[International Ornithologists' Union|International Ornithological Congress]] followed suit in 2022.<ref>{{Cite web|title=IOC World Bird List 12.1|website=IOC World Bird List Datasets|language=en-US|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.12.1}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Eaton|first=James A.|title=Birds of the Indonesian Archipelago: Greater Sundas and Wallacea|isbn=978-84-16728-44-2|oclc=1249090579}}</ref>
